attacking_style: Attacks from breakaways on final climbs with 2-3km remaining, typically after prolonged pacing work. Excels at high-altitude finishes (2000m+) and steep gradients where his mountain bike background shows. Patient, rarely attacks prematurely.
team_tactics: Primary mountain domestique setting devastating tempo in final 2km to isolate rivals before leader attacks. Occasionally freed for stage wins when team GC secured or compromised. Placed in early breaks to guarantee results (Vuelta 2021/2023 pattern).
terrain_preference: High-altitude finishes above 2000m are his signature strength. Excels on steep gradients (14%+) but vulnerable on longer sustained climbs (Angliru-type efforts). Mountain town finishes suit his physiology. Time trials are a significant weakness.
vulnerability: Struggles to follow accelerations on 23% ramps and sustained 4-5km+ climbs when already fatigued. Third Grand Tour of season exposed durability limits. Flat time trials far outside wheelhouse. Cannot match elite climbers on repeated steep accelerations.
key_patterns: Devastating final 2km pacing that immediately drops rivals, employed across 10+ mountain stages.
High-altitude specialist: all stage wins at 2000m+, consistently strongest above treeline.
Transitioned from super-domestique to GC winner in 2023, but teammates exposed steep gradient vulnerability.
Freed for stage hunting when leader abandons or secured, immediately pivots to personal ambitions.
Positioned in early breaks for tactical insurance, guaranteeing Jumbo-Visma results regardless of peloton outcome.
Sprints for bonus seconds against GC rivals when team tactics require, prioritizes team over personal placement.
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta GC win despite third GT showing career peak, though exposed vulnerability on steepest gradients suggests limits against fresher elite climbers in sustained battles
